medical_history.chief_complaint: Hemifacial asymmetry with flattening of the right forehead and limited opening of the mouth with trismus
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: A few months after birth, her parents noticed hemifacial asymmetry with flattening of the right forehead and limited opening of the mouth with trismus. At the age of 1 year, the patient was treated for TMJ arthrosis in another center, where she underwent surgery consisting in masseter muscle weakening. This operation was unsuccessful.
medical_history.past_medical_history: TMJ arthrosis, masseter muscle weakening surgery at age 1
medical_history.family_history: No history of trauma to the mandible or familiar occurrence of similar abnormalities

physical_examination.general_examination: Mandibular lock with incisal clearance of 5 mm, associated with extremely limited protrusion and lateral mandibular movements. On palpation of the masticatory muscles, the masseter was found to be enlarged and very tense.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Preoperative three-dimensional CT reconstructions showed an enlarged coronoid process protruding at least 1 cm above the inferior rim of the zygomatic arch, accompanied by bone sclerosis. The zygomatic bone had an abnormal shape with an excess in the lower part.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Hyperplastic growth of the coronoid process with reactive new woven bone formation. Biopsy from the temporal bone consistent with a hyperplastic reactive process, with no evidence of inflammation. The periosteum of the temporal region showed dense fibrotic tissue, with rare sparse regenerating fibers of striated muscles; the bone showed cortical lamellar tissue including some medullary spaces containing hematopoietic marrow. The resected medial portion of the masseter muscle was hypertrophic.

radiographic_examinations.findings: An enlarged coronoid process protruding at least 1 cm above the inferior rim of the zygomatic arch, accompanied by bone sclerosis. The zygomatic bone had an abnormal shape with an excess in the lower part.
Confirmed the resected area and partial removal of the lower zygomatic body.

other_tests.findings: Mandibular lock with incisal clearance of 5 mm, associated with extremely limited protrusion and lateral mandibular movements. Restricted translation of the TMJ was possible with the mouth kept in a strained open position. Intraoral examination revealed a well-aligned class 1 occlusion. No clinical evidence of infection or scarring. On palpation of the masticatory muscles, the masseter was found to be enlarged and very tense.

Coronoidectomy with fasciotomy and myotomy, muscular stripping and stretching, forced mouth opening, repositioning and plating of the zygomatic arch, resection of the lower border of the enlarged malar body.

other_tests.changes_over_time: Mouth opening after an 18-month follow-up was 35 mm (May 2009).

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on clinical features such as restricted mouth opening, facial asymmetry, and deviation on mouth opening. Preoperative three-dimensional CT reconstructions showed an enlarged coronoid process protruding above the inferior rim of the zygomatic arch, accompanied by bone sclerosis. Histological examination revealed hyperplastic growth of the coronoid process with reactive new woven bone formation.
